All charts —
USD/CAD (Bilateral)
CAD per USD
Bilateral exchange rate. Higher = weaker CAD.
Financial_Dd_Ceer
CEER (Trade-Weighted CAD)
%
[Coming soon] BoC's Canadian-Dollar Effective Exchange Rate. Methodologically better for inflation pass-through than bilateral USDCAD.
Financial_Dd_Spreads
WTI − WCS Differential
USD/barrel
WTI monthly mean (Alberta Economic Dashboard) minus WCS monthly (CAPP/Alberta). Normal range ($10–15/bbl) reflects pre-2018 average differential before pipeline constraints. Differentials above $20 historically coincide with pipeline take-away binding; Trans Mountain Expansion (TMX) opening in May 2024 added ~590,000 bbl/d capacity and materially compressed the spread. Alberta royalties scale with realized WCS price.
Financial_Dd_Equity
2-Year Yields
%
2-year benchmark government bond yields. Toggle 'Canada 2Y − Overnight' for a directional read of the market-implied BoC path — negative = pricing net cuts, positive = pricing hold or hikes. The 2Y embeds a term premium, so this is directional only; not a precise basis-point forecast. Treat with extra caution during QE/QT transitions or stress regimes when premium movements distort the signal. See the BoC's Financial Stability Indicators for model-decomposed estimates. 'Canada 2Y − US 2Y' is the cross-country differential (negative = Canadian rates lower than US, broadly CAD-negative).
Financial_Dd_Fx_Risk
Oil Prices
USD/barrel
Crude oil prices, USD per barrel. WTI and Brent are daily; WCS (Western Canada Select) is monthly. WTI briefly traded negative in April 2020 (futures contract anomaly); not shown.
About this dashboard — Built by jayzhaomurray using public data from Statistics Canada, the Bank of Canada Valet API, the Federal Reserve (FRED), and the Alberta Economic Dashboard. Raw data available as CSV in the GitHub repository. Section blurbs are AI-generated from public data using a fixed analytical framework verified against primary sources. Not investment advice.
